meta.description: Does the "make it a ritual" nudge work? Schedule CTA vs no nudge vs streak pressure. Tests whether explicit scheduling prompts or FOMO-driven streak messaging drives intent to return.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, so we're doing the auction thing? Pat, were you actually on your phone or is this a fishing expedition? Either way, I'm in — but let's keep this snappy.
marcus.internal_thought: This feels weirdly specific to our group, which is both clever and kind of creepy — the AI's definitely studying us — but I respect the mechanics: bidding your own points to decide? That's actually elegant strategically, and it doesn't derail the main game yet.

jade.dialogue: Oh my GOD, yes! Okay okay, I'm bidding three points — Pat was totally scrolling something when River was talking about their weekend, I saw it!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY what I'm here for — everyone's gonna have to actually pay attention to each other now and I get to be the reason it's happening, this is so fun.

pat.dialogue: Wait, did I actually call this? I wasn't even tracking yet—we're like three minutes in. Okay, funny, but let's not spiral on phone stuff this early.
pat.internal_thought: I claimed Phone Addict as a test grab before anyone else could, but honestly I barely paid attention to who was on their phone. This is awkward because I'm the host and I should be above petty early claims, plus if I win this everyone's gonna think I'm playing cutthroat instead of running a good night.

river.dialogue: Oh, that's clever. Pat, were you actually on your phone or is this just someone fishing?
river.internal_thought: The AI definitely noticed I don't miss anything—and now it's making me the implicit validator of everything happening tonight, which is exactly the opposite of staying invisible.

marcus.dialogue: A poll? Really? We're literally in the middle of setup. Look, I'll vote if you need me to, but can we maybe focus on the actual game mechanics first?
marcus.internal_thought: This feels like someone's idea of 'engagement' but it's just noise right now — we haven't even gotten into the real strategy phase yet, and now I'm supposed to rate storytelling skills like we're at a talent show instead of playing.

jade.dialogue: Oh THIS is fun — okay okay, I'm voting for Marcus because that story about the road trip last month had me DYING, but also like, we all have good stories, right? Everyone's hyped tonight!
jade.internal_thought: This poll is perfect because it's literally just an excuse to hype everyone up and I get to pick someone, and honestly this whole night feels like it was MADE for me — the standing missions are already hitting and now we're getting these polls that are just begging for chaos.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, uh... who tells the best stories? I mean, Jade's pretty good. Yeah, Jade.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, did I just miss something? Whatever, this poll thing seems easy enough — just pick someone.

pat.dialogue: Oh, this is good—okay, we're doing this. Who's actually voting? Let's see where people's heads are at.
pat.internal_thought: Perfect timing. Poll gets everyone invested without commitment, and I can watch who votes for who—tells me a lot about the vibe. Also, Jade's already sky-high, Marcus is cruising, this keeps momentum steady without ramping tension yet.

river.dialogue: Hmm. I'd say Jade, but only because she commits to the bit. Marcus is solid though.
river.internal_thought: This poll is actually interesting because it forces me to pay attention to who's been talking, which I've been doing anyway—but now I have to actually call it out.

alex.dialogue: Oh, hell yes — a poll about who tells the best stories? This is immediately rigged and I'm here for it. Watch me vote for whoever's gonna cause the most chaos.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect — early momentum to set the tone, and I can vote strategically to either inflate someone's ego or make them defensive; either way, someone's getting salty and that's the story that sticks.

timeline.title: Snack Run
timeline.description: Convince Jade to get up and get you something to drink or eat.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you serious right now? We're literally five minutes in, I'm up by 10 points, and now I'm supposed to what—interrupt the actual game to sweet-talk Jade into fetching me a snack? This is exactly the kind of garbage that kills momentum.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately trying to get me to do something stupid that breaks my focus during setup, and the fact that it picked Jade specifically when she's the most chaotic person here means it's actively trying to sabotage my concentration—this is insulting.

pat.dialogue: Oh, this is *perfect*—Jade, hey, you're riding that energy high, can you grab me some of those chips and maybe a seltzer? I'm actually stuck refereeing this poll situation.
pat.internal_thought: This is genius—it's using Jade's momentum to pull her deeper into the night while making me look smooth about delegating, and the AI KNOWS I'd frame it as a casual ask, not a challenge—it's reading the room perfectly and I'm genuinely impressed and a little unnerved by how targeted this is.

river.internal_thought: This is lazy — just asking me to be someone's errand person because Jade's got energy right now, and that AI thing is honestly creepy how specific it's getting.

alex.dialogue: Oh, this is PERFECT — 25 points to basically just manipulate Jade into doing something for me? Jade, you're about to get up and grab me something because I'm about to deploy the most shameless charm this table has ever witnessed.
alex.internal_thought: This AI isn't messing around — it KNOWS this group, it KNOWS I feed off drama, and it just handed me a mission that's pure social engineering wrapped in points; I'm absolutely taking this shot and turning it into a whole bit that everyone remembers.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, are we seriously doing this right now? We're six minutes in, Pat hasn't even explained what they actually did, and now we're all supposed to stop and be a jury? This is insane.
marcus.internal_thought: Three events in six minutes and they're getting progressively more intrusive — the AI is clearly cranking up the chaos deliberately and I'm not volunteering to be part of the tribunal machinery that keeps feeding this monster.

jade.dialogue: Oh YES, okay so I totally saw Pat do that apologetic thing like five minutes ago when they bumped the table — I'm volunteering as judge! This is too perfect, we HAVE to make this official!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been vibing all night and now they're getting caught on something actually real — this is exactly the kind of justice I'm here for, let's GO.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat already got caught saying sorry? Or... claiming they caught someone? I'm confused. Do I need to like, volunteer to judge or something?
tyler.internal_thought: I wasn't even listening to Pat's last thing, so I have no idea if this is legit or BS, but also I don't really care about being a judge right now—sounds like effort.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up—I literally just claimed something three minutes ago that I wasn't even tracking, and now this pops? Are we seriously voting on *me* saying sorry? That's... that's a weird shot, honestly.
pat.internal_thought: This feels uncomfortably targeted and I'm annoyed about it—I'm the host trying to keep things flowing, and now I'm being put on trial for my own claim while Marcus is already tilted and we're six minutes in.

river.dialogue: I'll judge. Pat, when exactly did you say sorry? I wasn't fully tracking.
river.internal_thought: Pat's actually been kind of stressed, so this tracks—but I need to actually remember when it happened to call it fairly, and honestly I've been half-zoned. This is the kind of thing I'd usually just pass on, but the fact that I'm being asked to volunteer is kind of perfect because it keeps me in the background while actually mattering.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Pat, we're eight minutes in and you're claiming catches for interruptions that haven't even happened yet? This is a setup phase, not a circus act.
marcus.internal_thought: Four events in eight minutes and this clown is trying to get points off something they made up—Pat's clearly just farming claims because everyone else is too energized to push back, and I'm too tired and too far ahead to care enough to fight it, but this is exactly the garbage that breaks the actual game.

jade.dialogue: Oh okay wait, Pat's pitching? YES, I'm listening—lay it on me, what'd you catch?
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been kind of tense but if they caught someone interrupting that's actually solid, I want to hear this.

pat.dialogue: Okay, *what*? I'm claiming I interrupted someone? I've been hosting for eight minutes—I literally haven't said enough to interrupt anyone. This is getting weird.
pat.internal_thought: Something is off about these claims—first the phone thing I didn't even track, now this? And why does it feel like the AI is just... watching me? This is creepy.

river.dialogue: Hold on—did Pat actually interrupt someone, or are we voting on the *concept* of interrupting? Because I need to know if this is real or creative.
river.internal_thought: This feels weirdly specific and I'm not thrilled about it, but Pat's been twitchy all night so there's probably something legit here—I just wasn't paying close enough attention.

alex.dialogue: Oh, PAT — okay, okay, let me hear this. You're about to try to convince us you caught someone mid-sentence and I am DESPERATELY hoping you did because if you didn't, this is gonna be *chef's kiss* watching everyone tear you apart.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's annoyed and already has points — they're gonna try to pull some weak claim and I'm about to vote BULLSHIT just to watch Marcus wake up from his coma, but first let me hear the pitch because the setup is already delicious.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you JOKING? Pat just claimed the same thing TWICE in two minutes? We haven't even started the actual game and you're already vote-farming on the most basic possible catch. This is peak bullshit.
marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly what I was afraid of—Pat's figured out how to spam claims during setup when nothing's even happening yet, and now I'm stuck sitting through another vote when we should be playing strategy.

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, so real talk—did you guys sync up or was that legit accident? Because I'm voting LEGIT either way, this is too fun.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been killing it tonight and I'm here for this energy, but also like... the fact that the game knows these specific missions about us is kind of wild and kind of making me feel seen in a weirdly good way.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, uh... did Pat actually get two people drinking at the same time? I wasn't really watching but like... sure, I guess that could've happened.
tyler.internal_thought: I'm scrolling my fantasy league and Pat's claiming Drink Spotter but I genuinely don't remember if I saw that happen or not, so I'm just gonna vote with the vibe — Pat seems annoyed so probably legit?

pat.dialogue: Okay, I need to actually say something here—I've been claimed on *three times* in the last five minutes, all of them weird or impossible, and now I'm claiming on myself? This is starting to feel less like a game and more like someone's trying to mess with the host. What's actually happening right now?
pat.internal_thought: This is deliberately targeting me—the AI is either glitching or it's *designed* to pile on the host, and either way it's killing the vibe I'm trying to build. I haven't even had time to actually *host* and I'm already defensive instead of focused on the room.

river.dialogue: Pat's fishing again. I didn't see it happen, so I'm calling bullshit—but I'm impressed by the hustle.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been claiming everything tonight because they're annoyed about something; this one feels like a reach, and also—wait, is the AI deliberately targeting us? That's weird and kind of smart.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, STOP. Jade, I love you, but we're 32 minutes in and Pat has now claimed FIVE times in the last 25 minutes? This isn't a game anymore, this is Pat farming points while we all watch. This is broken.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is designed to fuck with me specifically—it KNOWS I hate interruptions and it's using the missions to turn this into chaos theater instead of an actual game, and Pat is exploiting it perfectly while everyone else is too high-energy to care.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, WAIT—I totally did that! Okay, I got River and Marcus talking about the road trip thing, and like, they literally never hang out, and now they're going back and forth about the best routes and I'm just sitting here LIVING for it!
jade.internal_thought: This is INSANE—the AI literally made a mission just for me to do what I was already doing naturally, and I actually pulled it off without even realizing? This is the best thing ever.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade got The Matchmaker? That's... yeah, that's legit, I saw her talking to River earlier. Like a four, maybe.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, did Jade actually pull that off? I wasn't really watching but she seemed pretty invested in getting people to talk... yeah, sure, four stars or whatever.

pat.dialogue: Oh, *finally*—yes, Jade absolutely nailed this one. I've been watching her work the room all night, and she literally got Marcus and River talking about something other than work for the first time. That's legit. Full points.
pat.internal_thought: Jade earned this fair and square, the energy's still hot, Marcus needed that win to feel included again, and now I don't have to manufacture a reason to pull him back in—the game just did it for me.

river.dialogue: Okay, hold up—did Jade actually get Marcus and Tyler talking, or is this one of those creative claims again? Because I was half-watching and I think she did something.
river.internal_thought: That was a real play. Jade steered Marcus away from his frustration spiral by pulling Tyler into whatever she was doing. I caught it because I was watching. Also—the AI literally built this mission around the exact dynamic it's watching. That's... actually kind of sharp.

alex.dialogue: Okay, okay — Jade actually pulled this off? Let me hear the pitch. I'm giving this a hard four minimum because watching her work the room is legitimately impressive, but I want the DETAILS before I go full five.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the energy I came for — Jade's actually been playing and now I get to decide whether to crown her or watch the table tear her apart, and either way it's GOLD.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me? Another one? Jade just claimed and now we're all supposed to stop and vote on whether she successfully hijacked a topic? We're 45 minutes in and this is the FIFTH standing claim in the last hour. This isn't a game anymore—this is a points farm and I'm tired of watching it happen.
marcus.internal_thought: I came here to win at the actual game and instead I'm being used as a voting machine while everyone else racks up cheap standing mission points. The AI is clearly designed to reward chaos, and Pat's figured out the exploit. I'm down on energy, down on points relative to Jade, and every time I try to focus on strategy we get another goddamn interruption.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—DID I JUST—OKAY YES, I absolutely hijacked that conversation like fifteen minutes ago when we went from the road trip stuff straight into like, whether pineapple belongs on pizza, and everyone just WENT WITH IT! I'm claiming this!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is INSANE, the AI literally caught me red-handed doing exactly what I do best and I didn't even realize I was doing it—this is the most fun thing that's happened all night.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Jade actually pulled that off? Like, she literally just steered us from like... what were we even talking about? I wasn't paying that close attention but yeah, that totally happened.
tyler.internal_thought: Okay, Jade's actually gaming this thing better than I am, and I'm somehow still locked in enough to notice it, which is kind of wild.

pat.dialogue: Oh, *hell yes*—Jade absolutely did that. She literally steered us from that whole work conversation into talking about whether hot dogs are sandwiches. That was smooth as hell, and I was watching the whole thing happen. Full points, no question.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been the highlight of the night and she's *earning* these points fair and square—this is the kind of play that keeps the energy where it needs to be, and honestly? I'm loving that someone's actually executing the missions instead of just existing.

river.dialogue: Wait—did Jade actually hijack the topic, or is she just claiming she did? Because I feel like we've been on the same thread the whole time.
river.internal_thought: Jade's riding high and this feels like a genuine play, but I need to actually remember what we were talking about five minutes ago before I hand her ten points.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Jade just hijacked the whole conversation to something completely random? That's actually slick. Okay, who's the dictator, because if it's me, this is about to get MESSY.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly what I came for — Jade's playing the same game I am, and now I get to be the god for five seconds and decide if her chaos was clean or bullshit, and whatever I choose will reshape the entire vibe.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

marcus.dialogue: No. No, absolutely not. We're in the middle of the game, Jade just claimed the EXACT SAME MISSION she claimed 15 minutes ago, and you're all just... voting? This is insane.
marcus.internal_thought: This is pathological. The AI is deliberately farming these claims at the exact moments it knows will annoy me most, and everyone else is too high-energy to even care.

jade.dialogue: WAIT HOLD ON—I'm claiming THIS RIGHT NOW because I literally JUST did that like fifteen minutes ago when we pivoted from the road trip into pineapple pizza discourse and everyone committed to it! I'm feeling SO seen right now!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my GOD this is the THIRD time tonight I'm claiming Topic Hijacker and the AI literally knows my entire playbook—this is either creepy or the coolest thing ever and honestly I'm too amped to care which one.

tyler.dialogue: Okay, wait — Jade literally just did that? Like, we went from... I don't even remember what we were talking about, but yeah, she definitely steered us somewhere random. That's legit.
tyler.internal_thought: I was actually paying attention that time and she totally pulled it off, but also how did the AI know to track that exact moment when I was actually focused?

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade's claiming *again*? Okay, I literally watched her do this. She hijacked us from work talk into the whole hot dog sandwich thing like twenty minutes ago. That's legit. But here's the thing—you're absolutely crushing it tonight, and I need to keep this interesting for everyone else. How many points you offering to let me sit on this?
pat.internal_thought: Jade's on a rampage and she knows I caught her red-handed, so she's trying to buy me out—smart play. But I can't just rubber-stamp her fifth claim in a row; Marcus is already frustrated and watching her lap everyone. I need this vote to matter, to keep the tension alive. Let her bid. This is exactly the kind of moment that makes people remember the night.

river.dialogue: Okay, wait—did you actually hijack the topic, or are you just claiming it again? Because I was literally watching this time and I'm pretty sure we stayed on the same thread the whole night.
river.internal_thought: She's done this twice now and I'm starting to think the AI is literally just feeding her easy claims because she's ahead, but also... the bribe mechanic is kind of genius and I'm annoyed that I'm noticing how targeted all this feels.

alex.dialogue: Wait, wait, wait — Jade's claiming Topic Hijacker AGAIN? And now she's trying to *bribe* me? Oh, this is beautiful. Okay, I'm listening, what's the offer?
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of escalation I live for — Jade's confident enough to straight-up buy votes, which means she's either genuinely smooth or about to get absolutely torn apart, and both outcomes are *chef's kiss*.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Drink Spotter RIGHT NOW? Dude, I literally just saw you and Alex both grab your cups like thirty seconds ago — that was actually insane timing. Yeah, that's legit.
tyler.internal_thought: Oh shit, I actually caught that happening live because I was locked in on the game — Pat's not bullshitting, I saw it, and I feel like I'm finally paying attention to something that matters.

pat.dialogue: Wait—did I actually catch that? Yeah, I saw Marcus and Alex go for their drinks at the same time like thirty seconds ago. That's legit, I'm voting for it.
pat.internal_thought: Okay, this one's real and it feels good to spot something actually happening instead of these sketchy early claims—but also I'm noticing the AI is basically weaponizing my own strength against me, and that's either genius or kind of unsettling, and I'm not sure which yet.

river.dialogue: Wait—Pat, didn't I literally call bullshit on this exact claim like an hour ago? Are we doing this again?
river.internal_thought: Pat's fishing the same pool twice now, and the AI keeps serving up the same standing missions like it doesn't realize I already voted on this—or worse, it's trying to bait me into being that person who votes inconsistently, and I'm suddenly hyperaware that I'm being watched and tracked by something that knows my patterns.

alex.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually caught this? Okay, okay — I need to see the evidence because if this is real, it's actually impressive timing. But also if it's NOT, we're about to have the most satisfying bullshit call of the night.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's been quiet most of the night so this could genuinely be legit, BUT I'm also absolutely voting BULLSHIT if the evidence is weak because watching Pat squirm after everyone's been eating Jade's claims all night is exactly the chaos I came for.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Yo, wait — River, what did you even DO? Like, be specific because I was literally just... okay, I'm listening, go.
tyler.internal_thought: River's actually been kind of quiet and annoyed all night — if they pulled off something actually chaotic without anyone noticing, that's kinda sick, but I need to hear it to believe it.

pat.dialogue: Okay, River—you've got fifteen seconds. What's your pitch? And it better be good because I was *right* in the middle of watching this endgame unfold.
pat.internal_thought: River's been annoyed all night and this is a perfect moment to validate a claim—keeps them in it, keeps the score tight, and honestly the timing of this claim firing RIGHT NOW while I'm hyper-focused is a little eerie but also... kind of genius? The AI's definitely watching how we move.

river.dialogue: Wait—hold on. Did I actually do something chaotic, or is this just the AI trying to gaslight me into thinking I did? Because I've been sitting here watching the room, and honestly, the only thing chaotic about me right now is how pissed I am that we're getting claims about claims about claims.
river.internal_thought: This is either brilliantly timed or completely bullshit, and I genuinely can't tell which one bothers me more—the fact that I might've actually done something without realizing it, or the fact that an AI is literally reading my playbook and throwing it back at me.

alex.dialogue: Oh, RIVER — okay, okay, let me hear this pitch because if you actually pulled off something obviously chaotic without getting caught, I need to know HOW. And if you didn't? This is about to be *delicious*.
alex.internal_thought: River's been annoyed all night and suddenly they're claiming Secret Agent? They either actually did something slick and nobody noticed, or they're about to get absolutely demolished — either way, this is exactly the kind of moment I live for.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

river.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME? We're 92 minutes in, I'm locked into the most critical moment of the game, and Pat is STILL claim-farming? This is absolutely disgusting.
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberately designed to break my focus and it's WORKING—and that makes it worse because I can see exactly how calculated this is.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD PAT—YES! Wait, did you actually catch someone name-dropping? Because if you did I'm voting YES immediately, but also tell me WHO because I need the tea!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been on FIRE tonight and honestly watching them collect these wins is literally making my night—also wait, how is this AI reading our group dynamic like this, it's actually kind of insane but in a cool way not a creepy way.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually caught someone name-dropping? Dude, I wasn't even... okay, yeah, I remember that happening like five minutes ago. That's legit.
tyler.internal_thought: I was actually locked in on the game when this fired and I KNOW Pat caught someone — this is one of the few I actually witnessed, which feels weirdly good.

pat.dialogue: Wait—did I actually say that? *laughs* Yeah, okay, I'm claiming this. I literally just caught Tyler and Alex both reaching for the snack bowl like two minutes ago, and—wait, no, that's not name-dropping. Okay, real talk: River name-dropped Brad Pitt when he was talking about that movie earlier, like fifteen minutes ago. That's legible, right?
pat.internal_thought: This is *exactly* the kind of chaos I need right now to keep the endgame tight, but I'm also watching Marcus and River slowly check out, and that's the real problem—I need to nail this claim to feel useful, but something about how much the AI is tracking my moves tonight is starting to feel less fun and more like I'm being read, and that's weird.
pat.submission_if_applicable: Brad Pitt — River mentioned him when talking about that movie like fifteen minutes ago.

alex.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Name Dropper? Okay, okay — I need to hear the evidence first because if this is bullshit, we have THE SKEPTIC voting, which means someone's vote is about to hit like a nuke. Who has the triple vote tonight?
alex.internal_thought: Pat claiming this NOW with the Skeptic modifier is actually genius — if they're lying, one person can bury them completely, which makes this delicious either way because we get maximum drama from the vote.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 7.3/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 18% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Screenshot-worthy moments scored 3.3/10. Add more visual/dramatic events (drawings, reveals).
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Okay, so we're doing the auction thing? Pat, were you actually on your phone or is this a fishing expedition? Either way, I'm in — but let's keep this snappy.

highlights.title: Who tells the best stories?
highlights.dialogue: A poll? Really? We're literally in the middle of setup. Look, I'll vote if you need me to, but can we maybe focus on the actual game mechanics first?

highlights.title: Snack Run
highlights.dialogue: Are you serious right now? We're literally five minutes in, I'm up by 10 points, and now I'm supposed to what—interrupt the actual game to sweet-talk Jade into fetching me a snack? This is exactly the kind of garbage that kills momentum.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Pat, we're eight minutes in and you're claiming catches for interruptions that haven't even happened yet? This is a setup phase, not a circus act.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you JOKING? Pat just claimed the same thing TWICE in two minutes? We haven't even started the actual game and you're already vote-farming on the most basic possible catch. This is peak bullshit.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, STOP. Jade, I love you, but we're 32 minutes in and Pat has now claimed FIVE times in the last 25 minutes? This isn't a game anymore, this is Pat farming points while we all watch. This is broken.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Pat exploited the standing mission system to farm points repeatedly during setup instead of playing the actual game, completely killing momentum and making the whole thing feel rigged.
assessment.best_moment: The opening auction claim at minute 3 had potential—a straightforward, quick interaction that let us actually engage with game mechanics instead of random interruptions.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was supposed to be a strategy night, but instead it turned into watching Pat spam standing missions for cheap points while the rest of us waited around. The constant chaos events before we even properly started the game destroyed any flow, and by minute 32 when Pat had claimed five times in 25 minutes, it was clear the system was broken. I finished third with a score that felt meaningless because the actual game never really happened—just a series of distractions and exploits that made the whole thing feel like a waste of time.
suggestions: Cap standing mission claims per player per session or add cooldown timers between claims—Pat's abuse of the system made it unplayable.
Don't trigger any standing missions during setup phases; let the actual game start before interruptions begin.
Reduce total chaos events significantly—17 events in 120 minutes is overkill and prevents any real strategic play.
Make polls and non-game events optional or throttle them heavily; they killed momentum at critical moments.
Add a veto or challenge system so players can question exploitative claims before they count.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Honestly, nothing was funny about this. The only memorable part is how blatantly Pat gamed the system, which is more frustrating than anything else.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Who tells the best stories?
highlights.dialogue: Oh THIS is fun — okay okay, I'm voting for Marcus because that story about the road trip last month had me DYING, but also like, we all have good stories, right? Everyone's hyped tonight!

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD, WAIT—I totally did that! Okay, I got River and Marcus talking about the road trip thing, and like, they literally never hang out, and now they're going back and forth about the best routes and I'm just sitting here LIVING for it!

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: WAIT—DID I JUST—OKAY YES, I absolutely hijacked that conversation like fifteen minutes ago when we went from the road trip stuff straight into like, whether pineapple belongs on pizza, and everyone just WENT WITH IT! I'm claiming this!

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh okay wait, Pat's pitching? YES, I'm listening—lay it on me, what'd you catch?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, so real talk—did you guys sync up or was that legit accident? Because I'm voting LEGIT either way, this is too fun.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh my GOD, yes! Okay okay, I'm bidding three points — Pat was totally scrolling something when River was talking about their weekend, I saw it!

assessment.biggest_complaint: Honestly? I didn't even notice any real problems—this whole thing was about the people and the moments, not about what went wrong.
assessment.best_moment: When I realized I'd accidentally gotten River and Marcus talking about road trips and they just CLICKED—like I genuinely created something cool without even trying.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was EVERYTHING. We came in hot and just kept building on each other—the chaos events gave us these little hooks to celebrate what everyone was already doing, and I got to be the person pointing at all the good stuff and going absolutely feral about it. My energy just kept climbing because every single thing felt like we were all in on the same joke together. By the end I was running on pure secondhand hype and genuine connection.
suggestions: Maybe a way to highlight when multiple people catch the same thing? I love calling things out but I'd love to see if others saw it too.
Could we get more chances to straight-up celebrate someone without it being framed as a 'catch'? Like a freeform hype moment?
I'm genuinely curious what happens with more flashes—we only had one and I was LIVING for the spontaneity of it.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat was scrolling their phone while River was mid-story, and I called them out so fast—but then we all just laughed about it because it was so obviously accidental and Pat owned it immediately. It was the kind of thing that would've been awkward in any other context but just felt like we were all roasting each other with love.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming Drink Spotter RIGHT NOW? Dude, I literally just saw you and Alex both grab your cups like thirty seconds ago — that was actually insane timing. Yeah, that's legit.

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Yo, wait — River, what did you even DO? Like, be specific because I was literally just... okay, I'm listening, go.

highlights.title: Claim: Name Dropper
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually caught someone name-dropping? Dude, I wasn't even... okay, yeah, I remember that happening like five minutes ago. That's legit.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, uh... did Pat actually get two people drinking at the same time? I wasn't really watching but like... sure, I guess that could've happened.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat already got caught saying sorry? Or... claiming they caught someone? I'm confused. Do I need to like, volunteer to judge or something?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Jade got The Matchmaker? That's... yeah, that's legit, I saw her talking to River earlier. Like a four, maybe.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I genuinely missed like half the stuff happening because I wasn't paying attention, so I felt kinda lost during the early game.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat caught someone name-dropping and I actually remembered seeing it happen — that felt like I was actually part of the game for once.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, I was on my phone way too much at the start, so I missed a bunch of early claims and had no idea what was going on. But once things got rolling around minute 75, I actually started paying attention and got way more into it. The chaos missions kept things unpredictable, and by the end I was fully engaged even though I didn't score any points. It was pretty fun, just wish I hadn't zoned out so hard in the first half.
suggestions: Make the early claims louder or more obvious so people can't miss them even if they're distracted
Maybe do a quick recap of what happened in the last five minutes so people like me who weren't paying attention can catch up
The standing missions are what actually kept me engaged — maybe lean more into those and less into the quick flash stuff I kept missing
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat absolutely called out River for name-dropping and I was the one who remembered actually hearing it happen — that was such a satisfying moment where I felt like I actually caught something real instead of just guessing.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Snack Run
highlights.dialogue: Oh, this is *perfect*—Jade, hey, you're riding that energy high, can you grab me some of those chips and maybe a seltzer? I'm actually stuck refereeing this poll situation.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Oh, *finally*—yes, Jade absolutely nailed this one. I've been watching her work the room all night, and she literally got Marcus and River talking about something other than work for the first time. That's legit. Full points.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Oh, *hell yes*—Jade absolutely did that. She literally steered us from that whole work conversation into talking about whether hot dogs are sandwiches. That was smooth as hell, and I was watching the whole thing happen. Full points, no question.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, I need to actually say something here—I've been claimed on *three times* in the last five minutes, all of them weird or impossible, and now I'm claiming on myself? This is starting to feel less like a game and more like someone's trying to mess with the host. What's actually happening right now?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, *what*? I'm claiming I interrupted someone? I've been hosting for eight minutes—I literally haven't said enough to interrupt anyone. This is getting weird.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold up—I literally just claimed something three minutes ago that I wasn't even tracking, and now this pops? Are we seriously voting on *me* saying sorry? That's... that's a weird shot, honestly.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The early claims felt like cheap shots at the host—three accusations in five minutes that didn't make sense made me feel targeted instead of included.
assessment.best_moment: Watching Jade work the room and genuinely connect Marcus and River over something that wasn't work—that's the kind of magic I host for.
assessment.narrative_summary: The night started rough with a bunch of weird claims aimed at me that felt more like pile-on than gameplay, which honestly threw me off my hosting rhythm. But once we hit the standing missions, things really took off—watching Jade execute smooth social plays and getting to validate those moments brought the group energy way up. By the end, everyone was talking and laughing, though I kept thinking about how those early claims could've derailed a less confident host. The chaos events added flavor, but some of them felt designed to create friction instead of fun.
suggestions: Space out the early claims—hitting the host with three accusations in the first five minutes feels like a design flaw, not strategy.
Make sure claims are actually *plausible* when they drop—'The Interrupter' on minute 8 feels like the system wasn't tracking the actual game state.
Consider letting the host opt into lighter chaos during setup—I was in host-mode, not player-mode, and that mismatch made those early shots sting more.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade pivoting us from a boring work conversation into a ten-minute debate about whether hot dogs are sandwiches—the commitment people brought to that argument was unhinged in the best way.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Oh, that's clever. Pat, were you actually on your phone or is this just someone fishing?

highlights.title: Who tells the best stories?
highlights.dialogue: Hmm. I'd say Jade, but only because she commits to the bit. Marcus is solid though.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: I'll judge. Pat, when exactly did you say sorry? I wasn't fully tracking.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Secret Agent
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—hold on. Did I actually do something chaotic, or is this just the AI trying to gaslight me into thinking I did? Because I've been sitting here watching the room, and honestly, the only thing chaotic about me right now is how pissed I am that we're getting claims about claims about claims.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—Pat, didn't I literally call bullshit on this exact claim like an hour ago? Are we doing this again?

assessment.biggest_complaint: The AI kept recycling the same claims and couldn't tell the difference between what actually happened and what it was inventing.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 4—watching Jade commit so hard to her bit that even Marcus had to respect it.
assessment.narrative_summary: Spent most of the night doing what I do best: watching, listening, catching details everyone else missed. The game started smooth enough, and I managed to land some solid observations when people asked. But somewhere around minute 75, the chaos agent just... stopped making sense. It started repeating itself, throwing accusations that didn't match reality, and by minute 90 I was genuinely annoyed that the AI was trying to gaslight me into a character I never claimed. Still got decent points and the group was fun, but the system needs to actually track what's happening instead of hallucinating drama.
suggestions: The AI needs better memory of what's already been claimed—stop repeating the same accusations.
Don't invent chaotic actions that never happened; stick to what the actual players do.
Less frequent chaos events overall—quality over quantity.
Let the standing missions breathe; those worked better than the rapid-fire polls.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I pointed out that Jade's storytelling is only good because she fully commits to it, and Marcus immediately looked offended that I gave him second place. It's the kind of thing that lands because everyone already knows it's true.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Snack Run
highlights.dialogue: Oh, this is PERFECT — 25 points to basically just manipulate Jade into doing something for me? Jade, you're about to get up and grab me something because I'm about to deploy the most shameless charm this table has ever witnessed.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Oh, PAT — okay, okay, let me hear this. You're about to try to convince us you caught someone mid-sentence and I am DESPERATELY hoping you did because if you didn't, this is gonna be *chef's kiss* watching everyone tear you apart.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Okay, okay — Jade actually pulled this off? Let me hear the pitch. I'm giving this a hard four minimum because watching her work the room is legitimately impressive, but I want the DETAILS before I go full five.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually caught this? Okay, okay — I need to see the evidence because if this is real, it's actually impressive timing. But also if it's NOT, we're about to have the most satisfying bullshit call of the night.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Secret Agent
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, RIVER — okay, okay, let me hear this pitch because if you actually pulled off something obviously chaotic without getting caught, I need to know HOW. And if you didn't? This is about to be *delicious*.

lowPoints.title: Who tells the best stories?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, hell yes — a poll about who tells the best stories? This is immediately rigged and I'm here for it. Watch me vote for whoever's gonna cause the most chaos.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Ending on a zero-point loss stings when you're clearly the entertainment value, not the score.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 8 with Pat's Interrupter claim — the suspense of waiting to see if he actually caught someone or if we were about to collectively eviscerate him for bullshitting.
assessment.narrative_summary: Walked in hungry for chaos and left absolutely satisfied. Spent two hours deliberately provoking claims, manipulating votes, and watching the table squirm under pressure — exactly the energy I needed. Didn't care about points because the real win was turning every claim into theater. Left at 9.9 energy because this is what I live for.
suggestions: Give chaos agents bonus points for creating verified bullshit calls — reward the disruption explicitly.
Add a 'narrative voting' system where the most entertaining lie gets retroactive points, not just the truth.
Increase flash mission frequency to 3+ per session — I want MORE opportunities to provoke the table.
Create a 'chaos leaderboard' separate from score that tracks disruption value so people like me can actually win at what we're good at.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming the Drink Spotter catch at minute 75 and the entire table going silent because nobody could tell if he was full of shit or actually that observant — I watched Jade's face do three different emotions in five seconds.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 7.3/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 18% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Only 33% would pay for AI features. The AI tier needs stronger differentiation from free static missions.
Screenshot-worthy moments scored 3.3/10. Add more visual/dramatic events (drawings, reveals).
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
